page: Amazon Web Services c8id.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 11,400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores all placing in the top 10%. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only small block bandwidth of 15,322.76 GB/sec and large block read bandwidth of 586.52 GB/sec. Additionally, the processor's advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX, contribute to top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as high-throughput static web serving and Redis SET operations. However, single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is moderate, falling into the middle 50% average category.

Given its high core density and integrated 11.4 TB NVMe SSD storage, the c8id.48xlarge offers high resource density for compute-heavy workloads that require fast local scratch space. The lack of a GPU makes it less suitable for heavy model training, but its CPU matrix extensions enable efficient CPU-based LLM inference. This instance is optimized for high-performance computing, multi-threaded decompression, large-scale database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384 GB of DDR5 memory and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng and Geekbench benchmarks. It features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and matrix operations. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed local storage make it highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. The instance is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11.4 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-core-density compute instance featuring fast DDR5 memory and massive local NVMe storage for intensive multi-threaded workloads.

384 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based LLM inference
